content: Just as language learners use repeated deliberate reflection on their own writing to internalize grammatical and rhetorical patterns, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be adapted to borrow the spaced, scaffolded reflection framework from high school language arts learning to improve their ability to correct persistent output errors. This framing turns AI self-reflection from a niche post-generation validation step into a iterative skill-building process analogous to how humans learn through structured self-feedback.
